name: Skills Bundle
description: Installs and manages Code Workbench skill files (slash-command scripts). PRIMARY distribution is per-worktree session-launch injection (worktreeAssets.ts — see worktree-assets card): every Claude session launch refreshes <worktree>/.claude/skills from the bundle, manifest-guarded so foreign/tracked files are never clobbered, hidden from git via .git/info/exclude. Explicit installs into the active worktree's .claude/skills/ or ~/.claude/skills/ (user scope) remain via installWorkbenchSkills. Skill definitions live in the shared @code-workbench/mcp-core skills module — one .cjs file per skill under skills/, assembled by the skills.cjs index — with each skill's SKILL.md content embedded as a string so skills work offline and version-lock with the release. Drift detection (checkWorkbenchSkills) still guards EXPLICIT installs: user scope is byte-compared on activation and prompts to update (never auto-writes at user scope).
guidelines: installWorkbenchSkills must return { installed, removed } so the caller can surface a clear notification.
Support both 'user' scope (~/.claude) and 'project' scope (active worktree) to let users choose where skills live.
Each skill is its own packages/mcp-core/skills/<name>.cjs file exporting { name, body }; to add a skill create that file and register it in BUNDLED_SKILLS in skills.cjs.
skills.cjs is only the index — it requires the per-skill files and assembles BUNDLED_SKILLS; skills.mjs is a re-export shim for ESM consumers.
Consumers import the @code-workbench/mcp-core/skills package specifier; esbuild follows the require chain and inlines every per-skill file at build time.
Skill body text must reference MCP tools by their cw- server name only (e.g. mcp__cw-ast__find_duplicates). There is exactly one ast server — do not offer a cwb-ast fallback.
Keep skills repo-agnostic — the workbench is an IDE used across projects, so skill text must resolve paths against the active workspace and not assume the workbench's own codebase.

Drift detection: checkWorkbenchSkills(targetPath) byte-compares each installed SKILL.md against the embedded bundled body and returns { installedAny, stale, missing, legacy }. It is READ-ONLY — never write from a check path; installation stays in installWorkbenchSkills.

User-scope (~/.claude) is prompt-only: promptSkillsDrift prompts on activation, only where installedAny=true (never nag where the user never installed). Worktree scope is the opposite — silently refreshed each session launch through worktreeAssets.ts, which owns the never-clobber-foreign-files guarantee via its manifest.
A 'Not now' dismissal is persisted in globalState keyed by skillsBundleSignature() (sha256 over all skill names+bodies) so the prompt only returns when the shipped skill set actually changes.
anti_patterns: Do not silently overwrite customized skill files without versioning — check before clobber. (Worktree injection satisfies this via its ownership manifest: it only rewrites files it wrote itself.)
Do not auto-write ~/.claude (user scope) — user scope is explicit-install + drift-prompt only; automatic refresh is reserved for per-worktree injection.
Do not generate skill SKILL.md content inside arch-server.mjs — that responsibility was moved to the skills module (commit 556df47).
Do not import skills from a relative built path — use the @code-workbench/mcp-core package specifier.
Do not reference mcp__cwb-ast__ / mcp__cwb-tasks__ tools in skill text — the cwb- prefix is retired; only cw- servers are registered.
Do not fs-read skill .md files at runtime — skill bodies are embedded as strings so the bundle stays offline-capable.
decisions: Skills are bundled inside the extension rather than fetched remotely so they work offline and version-lock with the extension release.

Each skill lives in its own packages/mcp-core/skills/<name>.cjs file; skills.cjs is a thin index assembling BUNDLED_SKILLS — chosen over a monolithic file (escaping pain) and over real .md files (would break build-time inlining).
skills.mjs is a thin ESM re-export shim over the skills.cjs index, used by the VS Code extension.

BUNDLED_SKILLS ships cw-work, cw-plan, cw-implement, cw-review, cw-fix, cw-arch, cw-arch-audit, cw-duplicate-cleanup, cw-dead-code and cw-type-safety; consumers iterate the array so no count is hardcoded. Legacy folder names are removed on reinstall via LEGACY_SKILL_NAMES.

2026-07-03: Added skills drift detection to the VS Code extension: checkWorkbenchSkills + skillsBundleSignature in skillsBundle.ts; extension.ts prompts per scope on activation with an 'Update skills' action. Dismissals are remembered per bundle signature to avoid nagging.

2026-07-12: Switched primary distribution to per-worktree session-launch injection (worktreeAssets.ts), superseding the old 'never auto-inject' rule for WORKTREE scope only: skills/agents that name cw-code MCP tools should only be visible where that server is injected, and the manifest + git-exclude machinery preserves the never-clobber and clean-status guarantees. User scope stays prompt-only, and the old user-scope agents backfill was retired with one-time cleanup.
